content.problemStatement:
A king in a distant land wants to build a new palace for himself. There are some plots of land in the country. Some of them don't have any owner, some of the plots are owned by the citizens of the country. Being a good person, the king wants to pay the appropriate amount of money for the land he uses. If he uses some portion of a plot, he needs to buy the whole plot. Also, this is a weird country where the plots owned by the citizens may overlap. For an area, the king needs to buy all the overlapping plots to use that area. To use some area not owned by any citizen, the king doesn't need to spend any money.

Formally, the country is an M×NM \times NM×N grid with LLL plots owned by the citizens. Each of these plots is rectangular in shape. The ithi^{th}ith plot has its lower left corner at (xi,yi)(x_i, y_i)(xi​,yi​), length lil_ili​ (along with the xxx-axis), width wiw_iwi​ (along with the yyy-axis), and price pip_ipi​. The palace the king is going to build should be rectangular in shape as well. Please help him to find the rectangular place with the largest area which he can use without spending more than CCC amount of money.

(0,0)(0,0)(0,0) is the lower left corner of the country and (M,N)(M, N)(M,N) is the upper right corner. All the plots owned by the citizens are inside this area. The palace should be inside this area as well. The sides of the palace should be axis-parallel.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. Description of each test case is given below.
The first line of each test case contains three integers MMM, NNN, and CCC.
The second line contains LLL.
Each of next LLL lines contains the description of a plot owned by a citizen. ithi^{th}ith of these line has five integers: xix_ixi​, yiy_iyi​, lil_ili​, wiw_iwi​, and pip_ipi​.


Output Format
For each test case, print "Case i: ", and then the answer (mod 109+710^9 + 7109+7), where iii is the testcase number, 1-indexed. The answer should be the largest area of the rectangular shaped land that can be used without spending more than CCC amount of money.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The first line of each test case contains three integers MMM, NNN, and CCC. The second line contains LLL. Each of the next LLL lines contains the description of a plot owned by a citizen. ithi^{th}ith of these line has five integers: xix_ixi​, yiy_iyi​, lil_ili​, wiw_iwi​, and pip_ipi​.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The image above shows the lands owned by the citizens and the palace with area 12 (lower left corner at (0,0) and upper right corner at (4,3)).

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to find the largest rectangular area that can be used without spending more than CCC amount of money. We can iterate over all possible rectangular areas and calculate the cost of each area by checking which plots overlap with it. We can use a brute force approach to check all possible rectangular areas.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * M * N * L) — explain: per query is O(M * N * L), total is O(T * M * N * L)
solution.spaceComplexity: O(L) — explain: we need to store the plots
